channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Early voting begins in New York City mayor's race and contest to pick New Jersey's next governor
item.description: Polling places open Saturday for in-person voting in two of the year’s most closely watched elections: The New York City mayor’s race and the contest to pick New Jersey’s next governor.
item.dc:creator: Philip Marcelo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Shots fired, driver hurt and held after truck rams into Coast Guard base in California
item.description: A truck driver who backed into a U.S. Coast Guard base in the San Francisco Bay Area — the site of earlier protests against federal immigration agents — was shot by law enforcement officers and wounded, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Holmes Norton scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Thousands of curling irons recalled amid burn injuries
item.description: Thousands of curling irons sold at popular retailers, including on Amazon, are being recalled after reports of the curling iron barrel detaching, posing a burn risk.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The CEO of the Alamo's historic site has resigned after a top Texas Republican criticized her
item.description: The CEO of the nonprofit managing the Alamo has resigned after a powerful Republican state official criticized her and suggested she has views that are not compatible with the Texas shrine's history.
item.dc:creator: John Hanna, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Rosa Parks and Helen Keller statues unveiled at the Alabama Capitol
item.description: MONTGOMERY, Ala. (AP) — Statues of Rosa Parks and Helen Keller, pivotal figures who fought for justice and inspired change across the world, were unveiled Friday on the grounds of the Alabama Capitol.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DOJ prepares to send election monitors to California, New Jersey following requests from state GOPs
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Department of Justice is preparing to send federal election observers to California and New Jersey next month, targeting two Democratic states holding off-year elections following requests from state Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Richard Mille watches worth 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' stolen during F1 weekend
item.description: “The group targeted wealthy individuals that were wearing high-end luxury watches,” court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: New York adds $11M to hunger programs amid SNAP cuts, government shutdown
item.description: Governor Kathy Hochul announced $11 million in state funding for emergency food relief programs and urged the USDA to release $700 million in SNAP benefits to prevent a public health crisis, while the Fiscal Policy Institute suggested the state use its budget surplus to backfill the lost benefits.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US coffee prices spike due to tariffs and poor weather
item.description: It’s getting more expensive for Americans to get their caffeine fix. The average U.S. price of a pound of ground coffee hit $9.14 in September, a 3% increase from the August average of $8.87 and 41% higher than in September 2024, according to U.S. government figures.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and CHRIS RUGABER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Woman arrested after admitting to fabricating burglary, assault story
item.description: Upon further investigation, detectives found inconsistencies between her statements and newly obtained evidence.
item.dc:creator: Luisa Barrios, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Urinary tract infections linked to contaminated food
item.description: Almost 1 in 5 urinary tract infections are connected to contaminated meat due to E. coli strains, according to a study.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, Fox 5

item.title: California wildlife officers euthanize several gray wolves after attacks on livestock
item.description: “This decision was not made lightly nor was it easy,” said Charlton H. Bonham, director of California’s Department of Fish and Wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: Palisades Fire suspect has courtroom outburst while pleading not guilty at arraignment
item.description: Jonathan Rinderknecht, 29, who is facing charges for allegedly starting what became the devastating Palisades Fire, reportedly had a courtroom outburst.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: The leader of Canada’s most populous province says he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Inflation hit 3 percent in September as energy prices squeezed households
item.description: Annual inflation rose to 3% in September as a jump in gasoline and energy prices pushed price growth to its highest level since January.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gov. Youngkin declares state of emergency over SNAP benefits funding
item.description: By declaring a state of emergency, he can "expend emergency funds for the health, welfare, and safety of Virginians." Gov. Glenn Youngkin said.
item.dc:creator: Randi Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Virginia Democrats will try to reshape US House districts in counter to Trump's redistricting push
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats are taking steps to redraw their state’s U.S. House districts, hoping to boost their party’s chances in next year’s midterm elections and counter President Donald Trump’s push for more partisan districts in Republican-run states.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nationwide auto theft ring leads to Bay Area car business: CA AG
item.description: (KRON) -- Forty felony charges were filed Thursday against eight people tied to a nationwide auto theft ring and "complex" altered vehicle identification number scheme. The suspects then sold the 19 stolen cars out of their Bay Area business, announced California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a Portland protester's frog outfit
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Seth Todd was wearing an inflatable frog costume while protesting outside the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement Building in Portland, Oregon, when a federal officer unleashed a torrent of chemical spray directly into the costume's air vent. Video of the incident on Oct.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 times the White House underwent major renovations
item.description: President Trump's construction of a grand ballroom attached to the White House — starting with the demolition of part of the East Wing this week — will not be the first time a president has left a lasting mark on the people's house.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 53 percent disapprove of White House East Wing demolition: Poll
item.description: A majority of Americans said in a new poll they disapprove of President Trump’s plans to demolish part of the East Wing in the White House to make room for his $250 million ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trail Blazers’ Billups, Heat’s Rozier among dozens charged in Mafia-linked gambling probe
item.description: Portland Trail Blazers head coach Chauncey Billups and Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier are among more than 30 people who have been charged in connection with schemes involving illegal sports betting and rigged poker games backed by Mafia, authorities said on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE looking to hire over 40 health care workers
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security is hiring for more than 40 medical positions in its immigration detention centers across the nation.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation, Fox 5

item.title: Wyoming Capitol bomb investigators focus on men seen in security video
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming sought to identify two men they believe left a homemade bomb in front of the state Capitol, causing the building's evacuation after a passerby found the object and brought it inside. The bomb, at a time of heightened political angst in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walmart's 'Thanksgiving Meal Basket' will feed 10 people for under $40, company claims
item.description: The basket contains over 20 items (some canned, some pre-made, some fresh) including a turkey, potatoes, stuffing mix, fried onions, pie crusts, and boxed macaroni and cheese, among other ingredients.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9
